title: FC Makiyivvuhillya Makiyivka

infobox.value: Ukrainian Second League, Group B, 12th of 16 (withdrew)

was a Ukrainian football club from the city of [Nikopol](/wiki/Nikopol,_Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast"), [Dnipropetrovsk oblast](/wiki/Dnipropetrovsk_oblast "Dnipropetrovsk oblast") and [Makiivka](/wiki/Makiivka "Makiivka"), [Donetsk Oblast](/wiki/Donetsk_Oblast "Donetsk Oblast") before 2015. Another FC Nikopol has existed in Nikopol prior to relocation of FC Makiivvuhillya.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
several clubs that did not compete in the 2022–23 season and did not renew their membership.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [FC Makiyivvuhillya Makiyivka](/w/index.php?title=FC_Makiyivvuhillya_Makiyivka&redirect=no "FC Makiyivvuhillya Makiyivka"))

Football club

FC Nikopol

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| Founded | 2009 |
| Dissolved | 2022 |
| Ground | [Elektrometalurh Stadium](/wiki/Elektrometalurh_Stadium "Elektrometalurh Stadium"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) [Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast](/wiki/Nikopol,_Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast") |

|  | |

Not to be confused with [FC Nikopol (2007)](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol_(2007)&action=edit&redlink=1 "FC Nikopol (2007) (page does not exist)").

**FC Nikopol** was a Ukrainian football club from the city of [Nikopol](/wiki/Nikopol,_Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast"), [Dnipropetrovsk oblast](/wiki/Dnipropetrovsk_oblast "Dnipropetrovsk oblast") and [Makiivka](/wiki/Makiivka "Makiivka"), [Donetsk Oblast](/wiki/Donetsk_Oblast "Donetsk Oblast") before 2015. Another FC Nikopol has existed in Nikopol prior to relocation of FC Makiivvuhillya.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 21 June 2023 the 31st PFL Conference **excluded** several clubs that did not compete in the 2022–23 season and did not renew their membership.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Makiyivvuhillya (Makeyevugol)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Makiyivvuhillya (Makeyevugol)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/7/74/Makiivvuhillya.png/250px-Makiivvuhillya.png)](/wiki/File:Makiivvuhillya.png)

Emblem of Makiyivvuhillya (2009–2015)

It should be mentioned that previously Makiivvuhillya was represented by a team of the [Bazhanov coal mine](/wiki/Bazhanov_coal_mine "Bazhanov coal mine") known as [FC Shakhtar Makiivka](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Makiivka "FC Shakhtar Makiivka").

The current club was formed in [Makiivka](/wiki/Makiivka "Makiivka") in 2009 and became professional in 2011 entering the [Professional Football League of Ukraine](/wiki/Professional_Football_League_of_Ukraine "Professional Football League of Ukraine") and was based in [Makiivka](/wiki/Makiivka "Makiivka") under the name **FC Makiyivvuhillya Makiyivka**. The name of the club is associated Makiyivka's coal industry, since the city of Makiyivka is close proximity to the city of [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"). The football team was created out of the state mining enterprise ***Makiyivvuhillya*** in the summer of 2009 to participate in the Coal Industry Cup. In the finals of the Coal Industry Cup, a tournament which Makiyivka defeated SE "Dzerzhynskvuhillya."[[4]](#cite_note-Makiyivka-4)

After the event the company decided to continue supporting the football team,
with general director Stanislav Tolchin becoming the honorary president of football club.
The President of the club became Anatoly Akimochkin and head coach was Spartak Zhyhulin.[[4]](#cite_note-Makiyivka-4)

In the winter of 2009, Makiyivvuhillya won the city championship in Makiyivka and finished 3rd in the Donetsk oblast championship.
In 2010 the team became a finalist of the Coal Industry Cup (losing to [FC Shakhtar Sverdlovsk](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Sverdlovsk "FC Shakhtar Sverdlovsk")) and 3rd place again in the Donetsk oblast championship. In 2011, the team participated in the [Ukrainian Amateur championship](/wiki/Ukrainian_Football_Amateur_Association "Ukrainian Football Amateur Association").

In the Second League the club played its home games at the Bazhanovets Stadium, which was also used by the former Soviet/Ukrainian club [Shakhtar Makiyivka](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Makiivka "FC Shakhtar Makiivka") (also known as Bazhanovets) but the stadium was found lacking in facilities. Later the club played at Metalurh Stadium in [Yenakiieve](/wiki/Yenakiieve "Yenakiieve").

### Nikopol-NPHU

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Nikopol-NPHU")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/94/Nikopol-logo.jpg/250px-Nikopol-logo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nikopol-logo.jpg)

Logo used since 2015 till 2017.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/bd/Nikopol_city_gerb.png)](/wiki/File:Nikopol_city_gerb.png)

Nikopol city coat of arms

In 2014, the club was forced to relocate to [Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast](/wiki/Nikopol,_Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast"), due to the 2014 Russian aggression against Ukraine. It also lost its main sponsor Makiyivvuhillya, but in Nikopol the club was taken under its wing the Independent Trade Union of Miners of Ukraine (NPHU) which added own abbreviation to the club's name as *Makeyevugol-NPHU*. After the [2014–15](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Ukrainian_Second_League "2014–15 Ukrainian Second League") season the club formally withdrew from the PFL and reentered under the name of **FC Nikopol-NPHU**.[[5]](#cite_note-nphu-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The club plays at the stadium of [FC Elektrometalurh-NZF Nikopol](/wiki/FC_Elektrometalurh-NZF_Nikopol "FC Elektrometalurh-NZF Nikopol"), while trains at the stadium of FC Kolos Chkalove from Chkalove.

### FC Nikopol

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: FC Nikopol")]

During the winter break of the [2016–17 Ukrainian Second League](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Ukrainian_Second_League "2016–17 Ukrainian Second League") season, the club renamed themselves to **FC Nikopol.**[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Other clubs in Nikopol

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Nikopol&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other clubs in Nikopol")]

In Nikopol is another **FC Nikopol** that participated in the [2007 Ukrainian Amateur Cup](/wiki/2007_Ukrainian_Amateur_Cup "2007 Ukrainian Amateur Cup") and place third in [football championships of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast](/wiki/Football_Federation_of_Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Football Federation of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast") in 2009 and 2013. Both FC Nikopol and FC Kolos Chkalove were withdrawn from the [Dnipropetrovsk Oblast](/wiki/Dnipropetrovsk_Oblast "Dnipropetrovsk Oblast") championship in 2015.
